documentation.value:
Set the string value of a key.

```redis
SET mykey "hello"
SET mykey "hello" EX 60
SET mykey "hello" NX
```

Set key with TTL in seconds.

```redis
SET session:abc token123 EX 3600
```

Set key with TTL in milliseconds.

```redis
SET key val PX 5000
```

Set key only if it does not already exist.

```redis
SET lock:resource owner NX EX 30
```

Set key only if it already exists.

```redis
SET mykey newval XX
```

Set key to value if key does not exist. Returns 1 if set, 0 if not.

```redis
SETNX mykey "Hello"
```

Set key to hold value with TTL in seconds.

```redis
SETEX session:id 3600 "data"
```

Set key with TTL in milliseconds.

```redis
PSETEX mykey 100000 "val"
```

Get the value of key. Returns nil if key does not exist.

```redis
GET mykey
```

Atomically set key to value and return the old value.

```redis
GETSET counter 0
```

Get the value and delete the key atomically.

```redis
GETDEL tempkey
```

Get value and optionally set expiration.

```redis
GETEX mykey EX 100
GETEX mykey PERSIST
```

Set multiple key-value pairs atomically.

```redis
MSET key1 "Hello" key2 "World"
```

Get values of all specified keys.

```redis
MGET key1 key2 key3
```

Set multiple keys only if none exist.

```redis
MSETNX key1 "Hello" key2 "World"
```

Append a value to a key. Creates key if not exists.

```redis
APPEND mykey " World"
```

Returns the length of the string stored at key.

```redis
STRLEN mykey
```

Returns substring of the string value.

```redis
GETRANGE mykey 0 3
```

Overwrite part of string at key starting at offset.

```redis
SETRANGE mykey 6 "Redis"
```

Increment the integer value of a key by one.

```redis
INCR counter
```

Increment the integer value by a given amount.

```redis
INCRBY counter 5
```

Increment the float value of a key.

```redis
INCRBYFLOAT price 2.5
```

Decrement the integer value of a key by one.

```redis
DECR counter
```

Decrement the integer value by a given amount.

```redis
DECRBY counter 5
```

Deprecated alias for GETRANGE.

```redis
SUBSTR mykey 0 3
```

Find the longest common substring (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
LCS key1 key2
LCS key1 key2 LEN
```

Set field in hash to value.

```redis
HSET user:1 name "Alice" age "30"
```

Get value of a hash field.

```redis
HGET user:1 name
```

Set multiple fields in a hash.

```redis
HMSET user:1 name "Alice" age "30" email "alice@example.com"
```

Get values of multiple hash fields.

```redis
HMGET user:1 name age email
```

Get all fields and values in a hash.

```redis
HGETALL user:1
```

Delete one or more hash fields.

```redis
HDEL user:1 age email
```

Check if field exists in hash.

```redis
HEXISTS user:1 name
```

Get the number of fields in a hash.

```redis
HLEN user:1
```

Get all field names in a hash.

```redis
HKEYS user:1
```

Get all values in a hash.

```redis
HVALS user:1
```

Increment integer value of a hash field.

```redis
HINCRBY user:1 age 1
```

Increment float value of a hash field.

```redis
HINCRBYFLOAT product:1 price 1.50
```

completions.label: HSETNX
completions.detail: Set hash field if not exists

documentation.value:
Set hash field only if it does not exist.

```redis
HSETNX user:1 email "default@example.com"
```

Return random field(s) from a hash.

```redis
HRANDFIELD user:1 2 WITHVALUES
```

Incrementally iterate hash fields.

```redis
HSCAN user:1 0 MATCH name* COUNT 100
```

Insert values at the head of a list.

```redis
LPUSH mylist "world" "hello"
```

Insert values at the tail of a list.

```redis
RPUSH mylist "hello" "world"
```

Remove and return the first element.

```redis
LPOP mylist
LPOP mylist 3
```

Remove and return the last element.

```redis
RPOP mylist
RPOP mylist 3
```

Returns the length of a list.

```redis
LLEN mylist
```

Get a range of elements from a list.

```redis
LRANGE mylist 0 -1
LRANGE mylist 0 9
```

Get an element from a list by its index.

```redis
LINDEX mylist 0
```

Set element at index in list.

```redis
LSET mylist 0 "new_value"
```

Insert element before or after pivot.

```redis
LINSERT mylist BEFORE "pivot" "new"
```

Remove count occurrences of value.

```redis
LREM mylist 2 "hello"
```

Trim list to the specified range.

```redis
LTRIM mylist 0 99
```

Pop from tail of source and push to head of dest.

```redis
RPOPLPUSH source dest
```

Pop from one list and push to another (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
LMOVE source dest LEFT RIGHT
```

Blocking pop from list head.

```redis
BLPOP mylist 30
```

Blocking pop from list tail.

```redis
BRPOP mylist 30
```

Blocking version of LMOVE.

```redis
BLMOVE src dst LEFT RIGHT 30
```

Return position of element in list.

```redis
LPOS mylist "hello"
LPOS mylist "hello" RANK 2
```

Pop elements from first non-empty list (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
LMPOP 2 list1 list2 LEFT COUNT 3
```

Blocking version of LMPOP (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
BLMPOP 30 2 list1 list2 LEFT
```

Push to head only if list exists.

```redis
LPUSHX mylist "hello"
```

Push to tail only if list exists.

```redis
RPUSHX mylist "hello"
```

Add members to a set.

```redis
SADD myset "Hello" "World"
```

Remove members from a set.

```redis
SREM myset "Hello"
```

Get all members of a set.

```redis
SMEMBERS myset
```

Check if member is in set.

```redis
SISMEMBER myset "Hello"
```

Check if multiple members are in set (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
SMISMEMBER myset "one" "two" "three"
```

Get the number of members in a set.

```redis
SCARD myset
```

Remove and return random member(s).

```redis
SPOP myset
SPOP myset 3
```

Get random member(s) without removing.

```redis
SRANDMEMBER myset 3
```

Return the union of sets.

```redis
SUNION set1 set2
```

Store the union of sets.

```redis
SUNIONSTORE dest set1 set2
```

Return the intersection of sets.

```redis
SINTER set1 set2
```

Store the intersection of sets.

```redis
SINTERSTORE dest set1 set2
```

Return cardinality of intersection (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
SINTERCARD 2 set1 set2 LIMIT 10
```

Return the difference of sets.

```redis
SDIFF set1 set2
```

Store the difference of sets.

```redis
SDIFFSTORE dest set1 set2
```

Move member from one set to another.

```redis
SMOVE src dst "member"
```

Incrementally iterate set members.

```redis
SSCAN myset 0 MATCH h* COUNT 100
```

Add members with scores to a sorted set.

```redis
ZADD leaderboard 100 "alice" 200 "bob"
ZADD lb NX 100 "alice"
ZADD lb GT CH 150 "alice"
```

Remove members from sorted set.

```redis
ZREM leaderboard "alice"
```

Get score of member in sorted set.

```redis
ZSCORE leaderboard "alice"
```

Get scores of multiple members (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
ZMSCORE leaderboard "alice" "bob"
```

Get rank of member (0-based, ascending).

```redis
ZRANK leaderboard "alice"
```

Get rank of member (0-based, descending).

```redis
ZREVRANK leaderboard "alice"
```

Get the number of members in sorted set.

```redis
ZCARD leaderboard
```

Count members with score in range.

```redis
ZCOUNT leaderboard 100 200
ZCOUNT leaderboard -inf +inf
```

Count members in a lex range.

```redis
ZLEXCOUNT myset "[a" "[z"
```

Return members in sorted set by index range.

```redis
ZRANGE leaderboard 0 9
ZRANGE leaderboard 0 9 WITHSCORES
ZRANGE lb 100 200 BYSCORE LIMIT 0 10
```

Return members with score in range.

```redis
ZRANGEBYSCORE leaderboard 100 200
ZRANGEBYSCORE lb -inf +inf WITHSCORES LIMIT 0 10
```

Return members in lex range.

```redis
ZRANGEBYLEX myset "[a" "[z" LIMIT 0 10
```

Return members in reverse order.

```redis
ZREVRANGE leaderboard 0 9 WITHSCORES
```

Return members with score in range, descending.

```redis
ZREVRANGEBYSCORE lb +inf -inf WITHSCORES LIMIT 0 10
```

Increment the score of a member.

```redis
ZINCRBY leaderboard 10 "alice"
```

Store union of sorted sets.

```redis
ZUNIONSTORE out 2 zset1 zset2 WEIGHTS 2 1
```

Return union without storing (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
ZUNION 2 zset1 zset2 WITHSCORES
```

Store intersection of sorted sets.

```redis
ZINTERSTORE out 2 zset1 zset2
```

Return intersection without storing (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
ZINTER 2 zset1 zset2 WITHSCORES
```

Store difference of sorted sets.

```redis
ZDIFFSTORE out 2 zset1 zset2
```

Return difference without storing (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
ZDIFF 2 zset1 zset2 WITHSCORES
```

Return random member(s) from sorted set.

```redis
ZRANDMEMBER myset 3 WITHSCORES
```

Remove and return members with lowest scores.

```redis
ZPOPMIN leaderboard 3
```

Remove and return members with highest scores.

```redis
ZPOPMAX leaderboard 3
```

Blocking pop member with minimum score.

```redis
BZPOPMIN key1 key2 30
```

Blocking pop member with maximum score.

```redis
BZPOPMAX key1 key2 30
```

Pop from first non-empty sorted set (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
ZMPOP 2 zs1 zs2 MIN COUNT 3
```

Blocking version of ZMPOP.

```redis
BZMPOP 30 2 zs1 zs2 MIN
```

Store specified range from sorted set (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
ZRANGESTORE dst src 0 9
```

Incrementally iterate sorted set.

```redis
ZSCAN myset 0 MATCH h* COUNT 100
```

Delete one or more keys.

```redis
DEL key1 key2 key3
```

Delete keys asynchronously (non-blocking).

```redis
UNLINK key1 key2
```

Check if key(s) exist. Returns count of existing keys.

```redis
EXISTS mykey
EXISTS key1 key2 key3
```

Returns the type of value at key.

```redis
TYPE mykey
```

Rename a key. Overwrites destination if exists.

```redis
RENAME oldkey newkey
```

Rename key only if new key does not exist.

```redis
RENAMENX oldkey newkey
```

Find all keys matching pattern. **Use SCAN in production.**

```redis
KEYS *
KEYS user:*
KEYS h?llo
```

Incrementally iterate the key space.

```redis
SCAN 0
SCAN 0 MATCH user:* COUNT 100 TYPE string
```

Return a random key from the database.

```redis
RANDOMKEY
```

Set a timeout on key in seconds.

```redis
EXPIRE mykey 60
EXPIRE mykey 60 NX
```

Set timeout in milliseconds.

```redis
PEXPIRE mykey 60000
```

Set key to expire at Unix timestamp.

```redis
EXPIREAT mykey 1700000000
```

Set key to expire at Unix milliseconds.

```redis
PEXPIREAT mykey 1700000000000
```

Get the remaining TTL in seconds.

```redis
TTL mykey
```

Get remaining TTL in milliseconds.

```redis
PTTL mykey
```

Remove the expiry from a key.

```redis
PERSIST mykey
```

Get the expiration Unix timestamp (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
EXPIRETIME mykey
```

Get expiration in Unix ms (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
PEXPIRETIME mykey
```

Serialize the value stored at key.

```redis
DUMP mykey
```

Deserialize and restore a key.

```redis
RESTORE mykey 0 "\x00..." REPLACE
```

Get the internal encoding of a key's value.

```redis
OBJECT ENCODING mykey
```

Get reference count of value.

```redis
OBJECT REFCOUNT mykey
```

Get seconds since last access.

```redis
OBJECT IDLETIME mykey
```

Get help for OBJECT subcommands.

```redis
OBJECT HELP
```

Sort elements in a list, set, or sorted set.

```redis
SORT mylist
SORT mylist DESC LIMIT 0 10
SORT mylist BY weight_* GET # GET name_* STORE result
```

Read-only variant of SORT (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
SORT_RO mylist DESC
```

Move key to another database.

```redis
MOVE mykey 1
```

Copy a key to another key (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
COPY src dst REPLACE
COPY src dst DB 1
```

Alters the last access time of a key.

```redis
TOUCH key1 key2
```

Wait for replication to complete.

```redis
WAIT 1 1000
```

Wait for AOF sync on replicas (Redis 7.2+).

```redis
WAITAOF 1 1 1000
```

Get logarithmic access frequency (LFU policy).

```redis
OBJECT FREQ mykey
```

Add elements to a HyperLogLog.

```redis
PFADD visitors "user1" "user2" "user3"
```

Return approximate cardinality.

```redis
PFCOUNT visitors
PFCOUNT hll1 hll2
```

Merge multiple HyperLogLogs into one.

```redis
PFMERGE dest hll1 hll2
```

Set or clear a bit at offset.

```redis
SETBIT mykey 7 1
```

Returns the bit value at offset.

```redis
GETBIT mykey 7
```

Count the number of set bits.

```redis
BITCOUNT mykey
BITCOUNT mykey 0 0
```

Perform bitwise operation between keys.

```redis
BITOP AND dest key1 key2
BITOP OR dest key1 key2
```

Find position of first set/clear bit.

```redis
BITPOS mykey 1
BITPOS mykey 0 2 5
```

Perform arbitrary bitfield operations.

```redis
BITFIELD mykey SET u8 0 200
BITFIELD mykey GET u8 0
BITFIELD mykey INCRBY u8 0 10
```

Read-only variant of BITFIELD (Redis 6.0+).

```redis
BITFIELD_RO mykey GET u8 0
```

Append a new entry to a stream.

```redis
XADD mystream * name Sara surname Sanfilippo
XADD mystream MAXLEN ~ 1000 * field value
```

Return the number of entries in a stream.

```redis
XLEN mystream
```

Return entries in a range.

```redis
XRANGE mystream - +
XRANGE mystream 1526985054069-0 + COUNT 10
```

Return entries in reverse order.

```redis
XREVRANGE mystream + - COUNT 10
```

Read entries from one or more streams.

```redis
XREAD COUNT 10 STREAMS mystream 0
XREAD BLOCK 5000 COUNT 10 STREAMS mystream $
```

Blocking read from streams.

```redis
XREAD BLOCK 0 COUNT 10 STREAMS mystream $
```

Create a consumer group.

```redis
XGROUP CREATE mystream mygroup $ MKSTREAM
XGROUP CREATE mystream mygroup 0
```

Destroy a consumer group.

```redis
XGROUP DESTROY mystream mygroup
```

Remove a consumer from a group.

```redis
XGROUP DELCONSUMER mystream mygroup myconsumer
```

Set the last delivered ID of a consumer group.

```redis
XGROUP SETID mystream mygroup $
```

Read from stream as a consumer group member.

```redis
XREADGROUP GROUP mygroup myconsumer COUNT 10 STREAMS mystream >
```

Acknowledge stream messages.

```redis
XACK mystream mygroup 1526569495631-0
```

Transfer ownership of pending messages.

```redis
XCLAIM mystream mygroup myconsumer 3600000 1526569495631-0
```

Automatically transfer idle pending messages (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
XAUTOCLAIM mystream mygroup myconsumer 3600000 0-0 COUNT 10
```

Inspect the pending entries list.

```redis
XPENDING mystream mygroup
XPENDING mystream mygroup - + 10
```

Get information about a stream.

```redis
XINFO STREAM mystream
XINFO STREAM mystream FULL
```

List consumer groups.

```redis
XINFO GROUPS mystream
```

List consumers in a group.

```redis
XINFO CONSUMERS mystream mygroup
```

Trim the stream to a certain size.

```redis
XTRIM mystream MAXLEN 1000
XTRIM mystream MINID 1526569495631-0
```

Delete specific entries from a stream.

```redis
XDEL mystream 1526569495631-0
```

Set the last entry ID of a stream.

```redis
XSETID mystream 1526569495631-0
```

Listen for messages on channels.

```redis
SUBSCRIBE channel1 channel2
```

Stop listening from channels.

```redis
UNSUBSCRIBE channel1
```

Post a message to a channel.

```redis
PUBLISH mychannel "Hello World"
```

Subscribe to channels matching patterns.

```redis
PSUBSCRIBE news.*
PSUBSCRIBE h?llo
```

Stop listening from pattern channels.

```redis
PUNSUBSCRIBE news.*
```

List active channels with subscribers.

```redis
PUBSUB CHANNELS
PUBSUB CHANNELS news.*
```

Get subscriber counts for channels.

```redis
PUBSUB NUMSUB channel1 channel2
```

Get count of unique pattern subscriptions.

```redis
PUBSUB NUMPAT
```

Subscribe to shard channels (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
SSUBSCRIBE channel1
```

Unsubscribe from shard channels (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
SUNSUBSCRIBE channel1
```

Publish to a shard channel (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
SPUBLISH channel1 message
```

List active shard channels (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
PUBSUB SHARDCHANNELS
```

Get shard subscriber counts (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
PUBSUB SHARDNUMSUB ch1
```

Mark the start of a transaction block.

```redis
MULTI
SET key1 "val1"
SET key2 "val2"
EXEC
```

Execute all commands issued after MULTI.

```redis
EXEC
```

Discard all commands issued after MULTI.

```redis
DISCARD
```

Watch keys for conditional execution (optimistic locking).

```redis
WATCH mykey
MULTI
SET mykey newval
EXEC
```

Forget all watched keys.

```redis
UNWATCH
```

Execute a Lua script server-side.

```redis
EVAL "return redis.call('GET',KEYS[1])" 1 mykey
EVAL "return redis.call('SET',KEYS[1],ARGV[1])" 1 mykey myval
```

Execute a cached Lua script by SHA1.

```redis
EVALSHA sha1 1 mykey
```

Execute Lua script in read-only mode (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
EVALRO "return redis.call('GET',KEYS[1])" 1 mykey
```
Execute cached script in read-only mode (Redis 7.0+).

Load a Lua script into cache.

```redis
SCRIPT LOAD "return 1"
```

Check if scripts exist in cache.

```redis
SCRIPT EXISTS sha1 sha2
```

Remove all scripts from the cache.

```redis
SCRIPT FLUSH
```

Kill the currently executing Lua script.

```redis
SCRIPT KILL
```

Load a library (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
FUNCTION LOAD "#!lua name=mylib\nredis.register_function('myfunc', function(keys, args) return redis.call('GET', keys[1]) end)"
```

Call a Redis function (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
FCALL myfunc 1 mykey
```
Call a function in read-only mode (Redis 7.0+).

List loaded functions (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
FUNCTION LIST
FUNCTION LIST LIBRARYNAME mylib
```
Dump all loaded functions (Redis 7.0+).
Restore functions from dump (Redis 7.0+).
Delete all functions (Redis 7.0+).

Delete a function library (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
FUNCTION DELETE mylib
```
Get function execution stats (Redis 7.0+).

Add geospatial items to a sorted set.

```redis
GEOADD places 13.361389 38.115556 "Palermo"
GEOADD places NX 13.361389 38.115556 "Palermo"
```

Return distance between two members.

```redis
GEODIST places "Palermo" "Catania" km
```

Return Geohash strings of members.

```redis
GEOHASH places "Palermo"
```

Return longitude/latitude of members.

```redis
GEOPOS places "Palermo"
```

Search for geo members by radius or box (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
GEOSEARCH places FROMMEMBER "Palermo" BYRADIUS 100 km ASC COUNT 5
GEOSEARCH places FROMLONLAT 13.361 38.115 BYBOX 200 200 km ASC
```

Store GEOSEARCH results (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
GEOSEARCHSTORE dest src FROMMEMBER "Palermo" BYRADIUS 100 km
```

Query members in a radius. Use GEOSEARCH instead.

```redis
GEORADIUS places 15 37 200 km WITHCOORD WITHDIST COUNT 5 ASC
```

Query by member position. Use GEOSEARCH instead.

```redis
GEORADIUSBYMEMBER places "Palermo" 200 km
```

Get information and statistics about the server.

```redis
INFO
INFO server
INFO memory
INFO replication
INFO keyspace
```

Get the value of a configuration parameter.

```redis
CONFIG GET maxmemory
CONFIG GET *
```

Set a configuration parameter.

```redis
CONFIG SET maxmemory 256mb
CONFIG SET save "3600 1 300 100"
```

Reset the stats returned by INFO.

```redis
CONFIG RESETSTAT
```

Rewrite redis.conf with current in-memory config.

```redis
CONFIG REWRITE
```

Return the number of keys in the current database.

```redis
DBSIZE
```

Remove all keys from the current database.

```redis
FLUSHDB
FLUSHDB ASYNC
```

Remove all keys from all databases.

```redis
FLUSHALL
FLUSHALL ASYNC
```

Asynchronously save the dataset to disk (RDB).

```redis
BGSAVE
BGSAVE SCHEDULE
```

Asynchronously rewrite the AOF file.

```redis
BGREWRITEAOF
```

Synchronously save the dataset to disk. **Blocks server.**

```redis
SAVE
```

Return Unix timestamp of last successful RDB save.

```redis
LASTSAVE
```

Pause the server for N seconds (debug only).

```redis
DEBUG SLEEP 5
```

Synchronously save and shut down the server.

```redis
SHUTDOWN
SHUTDOWN NOSAVE
SHUTDOWN SAVE
```

Get entries from the slow log.

```redis
SLOWLOG GET 10
```

Get the number of entries in the slow log.

```redis
SLOWLOG LEN
```

Reset the slow log.

```redis
SLOWLOG RESET
```

Return the current server time.

```redis
TIME
```

List connected clients.

```redis
CLIENT LIST
CLIENT LIST TYPE normal
```

Return current connection ID.

```redis
CLIENT ID
```

Get the current connection name.

```redis
CLIENT GETNAME
```

Set the current connection name.

```redis
CLIENT SETNAME myconn
```

Kill a client connection.

```redis
CLIENT KILL ID 123
CLIENT KILL ADDR 127.0.0.1:6379
```

Pause all client commands.

```redis
CLIENT PAUSE 5000
```

Resume client processing.

```redis
CLIENT UNPAUSE
```

Set current connection to not be evicted (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
CLIENT NO-EVICT ON
```

Disable key touch for current connection (Redis 7.2+).

```redis
CLIENT NO-TOUCH ON
```

Listen for all requests received. **Impacts performance.**

```redis
MONITOR
```

Test if server is alive. Returns PONG.

```redis
PING
PING "hello"
```

Echo the given string.

```redis
ECHO "Hello World"
```

Change the selected database (0-15).

```redis
SELECT 1
```

Swap two Redis databases.

```redis
SWAPDB 0 1
```

Estimate memory usage of a key.

```redis
MEMORY USAGE mykey
MEMORY USAGE mykey SAMPLES 5
```

Get memory problems report.

```redis
MEMORY DOCTOR
```

Get detailed memory statistics.

```redis
MEMORY STATS
```

Ask allocator to release memory.

```redis
MEMORY PURGE
```

Show allocator internal stats.

```redis
MEMORY MALLOC-STATS
```

Return latest latency samples.

```redis
LATENCY LATEST
```

Return latency history for event.

```redis
LATENCY HISTORY command
```

Reset latency data.

```redis
LATENCY RESET
```
Render a latency graph for an event.

Get debugging information about a key.

```redis
DEBUG OBJECT mykey
```

Get total number of commands.

```redis
COMMAND COUNT
```

List all command names.

```redis
COMMAND LIST
```

Get details about specific commands.

```redis
COMMAND INFO GET SET
```

Extract keys from a command.

```redis
COMMAND GETKEYS SET mykey myval
```

Get docs for commands (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
COMMAND DOCS SET GET
```

List all users and their ACL rules.

```redis
ACL LIST
```

List all configured usernames.

```redis
ACL USERS
```

Get the ACL for a specific user.

```redis
ACL GETUSER default
```

Create or modify a user's ACL.

```redis
ACL SETUSER myuser on >password ~cached:* +get +set
ACL SETUSER myuser on >pass allkeys allcommands
```

Delete ACL users.

```redis
ACL DELUSER myuser
```

Return the current connection's username.

```redis
ACL WHOAMI
```

List command categories.

```redis
ACL CAT
ACL CAT string
```

Generate a secure password.

```redis
ACL GENPASS
ACL GENPASS 128
```

Show recent ACL violations.

```redis
ACL LOG
ACL LOG RESET
```

Save ACL rules to disk.

```redis
ACL SAVE
```

Load ACL rules from disk.

```redis
ACL LOAD
```

Authenticate to the server.

```redis
AUTH password
AUTH username password
```

Get cluster state information.

```redis
CLUSTER INFO
```

Get cluster nodes information.

```redis
CLUSTER NODES
```

Return the node's cluster ID.

```redis
CLUSTER MYID
```

Get mapping of cluster slots to nodes.

```redis
CLUSTER SLOTS
```

Get shard info (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
CLUSTER SHARDS
```

Add a node to the cluster.

```redis
CLUSTER MEET 192.168.1.1 6379
```

Remove a node from the cluster.

```redis
CLUSTER FORGET node-id
```

Make node a replica of another.

```redis
CLUSTER REPLICATE node-id
```

Trigger manual failover of the master.

```redis
CLUSTER FAILOVER
CLUSTER FAILOVER FORCE
```

Reset cluster configuration.

```redis
CLUSTER RESET HARD
CLUSTER RESET SOFT
```

Assign hash slots to this node.

```redis
CLUSTER ADDSLOTS 0 1 2 3
```

Remove hash slots from this node.

```redis
CLUSTER DELSLOTS 0 1 2
```

Bind/migrate a hash slot.

```redis
CLUSTER SETSLOT 5000 IMPORTING node-id
CLUSTER SETSLOT 5000 MIGRATING node-id
CLUSTER SETSLOT 5000 NODE node-id
```

Count keys in a hash slot.

```redis
CLUSTER COUNTKEYSINSLOT 5000
```

Return keys in a hash slot.

```redis
CLUSTER GETKEYSINSLOT 5000 10
```

Return the hash slot for a key.

```redis
CLUSTER KEYSLOT mykey
```

List cluster bus connections (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
CLUSTER LINKS
```

Delete all slots from node.

```redis
CLUSTER FLUSHSLOTS
```

Save cluster configuration to disk.

```redis
CLUSTER SAVECONFIG
```
Return failure reports count for a node.

Enable read queries on a replica.

```redis
READONLY
```

Disable read queries on a replica.

```redis
READWRITE
```

Make server a replica of another.

```redis
REPLICAOF host port
REPLICAOF NO ONE
```

Legacy alias for REPLICAOF.

```redis
SLAVEOF host port
SLAVEOF NO ONE
```

Internal replication command.

```redis
PSYNC replicationid offset
```
Internal replication configuration.

Initiate a coordinated failover (Redis 6.2+).

```redis
FAILOVER TO host port
```

Common cache-aside pattern with TTL.

```redis
# Check cache first
GET user:123
# On miss, set with expiry
SET user:123 '{"name":"Alice"}' EX 3600
```

Simple rate limiter using INCR + EXPIRE.

```redis
INCR rate:user:123
EXPIRE rate:user:123 60
# Check: if INCR result > limit, deny
```

Acquire a distributed lock with NX + EX.

```redis
SET lock:resource unique_id NX EX 30
# Release: check value then DEL
EVAL "if redis.call('get',KEYS[1]) == ARGV[1] then return redis.call('del',KEYS[1]) else return 0 end" 1 lock:resource unique_id
```

Common leaderboard using sorted sets.

```redis
ZADD leaderboard 100 "player1"
ZADD leaderboard 200 "player2"
ZREVRANGE leaderboard 0 9 WITHSCORES
ZRANK leaderboard "player1"
```

Store session data in a hash with expiry.

```redis
HSET session:abc user_id 123 role admin ip 10.0.0.1
EXPIRE session:abc 1800
HGETALL session:abc
```

Use list as a FIFO queue.

```redis
# Producer
LPUSH queue:tasks '{"task":"email","to":"user@example.com"}'
# Consumer (blocking)
BRPOP queue:tasks 30
```

Publish and subscribe to channels.

```redis
# Terminal 1 (subscriber)
SUBSCRIBE notifications
# Terminal 2 (publisher)
PUBLISH notifications "Hello subscribers!"
```

Send multiple commands in a pipeline.

```redis
# redis-cli --pipe or using client library pipeline
SET key1 val1
SET key2 val2
SET key3 val3
GET key1
GET key2
```

completions.label: Consumer Group Pattern

documentation.value:
Complete stream consumer group setup.

```redis
XGROUP CREATE mystream mygroup $ MKSTREAM
XREADGROUP GROUP mygroup consumer1 COUNT 10 BLOCK 5000 STREAMS mystream >
XACK mystream mygroup 1526569495631-0
```

completions.insertText:
XGROUP CREATE ${1:stream} ${2:group} \$ MKSTREAM
XREADGROUP GROUP ${2:group} ${3:consumer} COUNT ${4:10} BLOCK ${5:5000} STREAMS ${1:stream} >

completions.label: Lua Script Pattern

documentation.value:
Atomic compare-and-set with Lua.

```redis
EVAL "if redis.call('get',KEYS[1]) == ARGV[1] then redis.call('set',KEYS[1],ARGV[2]) return 1 else return 0 end" 1 mykey oldval newval
```

completions.insertText: EVAL "if redis.call('get',KEYS[1]) == ARGV[1] then redis.call('set',KEYS[1],ARGV[2]) return 1 else return 0 end" 1 ${1:key} ${2:expected} ${3:newval}

documentation.value:
Atomically transfer a key to another Redis instance.

```redis
MIGRATE host 6379 mykey 0 5000 AUTH pass COPY REPLACE
```
Enable/disable active expiry.

Reset the connection state.

```redis
RESET
```

Close the connection.

```redis
QUIT
```

Switch to RESP3 protocol.

```redis
HELLO 3
HELLO 3 AUTH username password
```

Load a Redis module.

```redis
MODULE LOAD /path/to/module.so
```

List all loaded modules.

```redis
MODULE LIST
```

Unload a Redis module.

```redis
MODULE UNLOAD mymodule
```

Load module with arguments (Redis 7.0+).

```redis
MODULE LOADEX /path/to/module.so CONFIG name value
```
